heroHeadline: Witkoff and Kushner Fly to Pakistan for Iran Nuclear Talks; Lebanon Ceasefire Extended 3 Weeks Under Hezbollah Protest
heroSubtitle: On April 25, US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner departed for Islamabad as Iran's FM Araghchi arrived via Pakistan for potential nuclear talks — Tehran publicly denied any direct meeting was planned, preserving calculated diplomatic ambiguity. The Lebanon–Israel ceasefire was extended 3 weeks on April 23, but Israel continued operations in south Lebanon on April 24 and Hezbollah declared the extension 'meaningless.' The DRC–Rwanda 5th Joint Oversight Committee met in Washington April 23; US officials described Rwanda's compliance as 'deeply disappointing.' 11 ceasefires nominally in force globally.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN, African Union, IGAD, Crisis Group, USIP, PA-X Database, and Tier 1–3 sources across all media poles.
